summary: LEARN OS Layer Testing Complete - All backend APIs pass (19/19). Frontend features work with 2 UI bugs identified (text contrast, click interception).

ui_bugs.issue: Very low text contrast on content card titles/summaries and guide content in Reader view - text is barely readable against dark background
Click interception by header element - content cards require force:true to click. The learn-header div intercepts pointer events from learn-content-card buttons

issues: Content card text (title, summary) has very low contrast - appears as faded text against dark background
Topic label colors are good but card content needs higher contrast
Time badges (1 min, 3 min video) are visible but main content is hard to read
Guide steps text has extremely low contrast - steps are barely visible
Summary and 'Watch for' sections have low contrast
Header and action bar have good contrast, but main content area needs improvement

action_items: CRITICAL: Increase text contrast in LearnPanel content cards - add explicit color to .learn-card-title and .learn-card-summary (currently inheriting faded styles)
CRITICAL: Increase text contrast in LearnReader guide content - the guide steps, summary, and watch_for sections need white/light text color
FIX: Content card click interception - the learn-header is intercepting clicks. Check z-index or pointer-events on the header vs content area
CSS FIX: Add 'color: white' or 'color: rgba(255,255,255,0.9)' to guide content text elements in LearnReader.jsx
critical_code_review_comments: LearnPanel uses inline styles but content card CSS classes (.learn-card-title, .learn-card-summary) need explicit color values
LearnReader guide-content, guide-section classes need explicit text color - currently too dark
The click interception suggests header has a z-index or sizing issue - may need overflow-hidden or pointer-events:none on certain header children
data-testid attributes are properly implemented across all interactive elements

success_rate.frontend: 85% - All features work but text visibility issues

seed_data_creation: 30 guides and 20 videos seeded in learn_guides and learn_videos collections
context_for_next_testing_agent: LEARN OS Layer testing complete. Backend APIs all working. Frontend needs CSS fixes for text contrast in LearnPanel (.learn-card-title, .learn-card-summary) and LearnReader (guide content). Also fix click interception on content cards.

features_tested.Topics grid displays correctly with 9 to: PASS - Grooming, Health, Food, Behaviour, Travel, Boarding, Puppies, Senior, Seasonal
features_tested.Clicking a content card opens the Reader: PASS (requires force:true due to click interception)
features_tested.Sticky action bar has 3 buttons: PASS - Let Mira do it, Ask Mira, Save

rca_of_issues.text_contrast: The LearnPanel and LearnReader components use CSS classes for text but don't have explicit color values. The dark theme background combined with inherited/default text colors results in very low contrast. Need to add explicit 'color: white' or similar to .learn-card-title, .learn-card-summary, and guide content elements.
rca_of_issues.click_interception: The learn-header element (position:fixed or similar) is intercepting click events from content cards below it. This happens when scrolling causes the cards to move under the header area. Need to check z-index stacking or add pointer-events:none to non-interactive header children.
